# HAMR Framework

> HAMR is a Go web framework built on Echo v4 for server-rendered HTML
> applications using templ + HTMX (with optional Alpine.js), backed by
> PostgreSQL or SQLite.
> Import path: github.com/FyrmForge/hamr

## Glossary

- **templ**: Go HTML templating language (NOT Go's html/template). Compiled to Go.
  Files use .templ extension. Components are Go functions returning templ.Component.
- **HTMX**: JavaScript library for HTML-over-the-wire. Attributes like hx-post,
  hx-swap, hx-target drive server interaction without writing JS.
- **OOB swap**: HTMX out-of-band swap. Element with hx-swap-oob="true" replaces
  a matching element by ID anywhere in the DOM, independent of the main swap target.
- **PRG pattern**: Post-Redirect-Get. POST handlers redirect with 303 to prevent
  form resubmission.
- **PHC format**: Password Hash Competition format string for Argon2id hashes.
- **Echo context (echo.Context)**: The request/response wrapper in Echo v4.
  NOT Go's context.Context. Use c.Request().Context() for the stdlib context.
- **functional options**: Constructor pattern using variadic Option functions
  (e.g., server.New(server.WithPort(8080))).
- **Store interface**: Repository pattern used in scaffolded projects.
  Generated projects define a Store interface in their internal/repo/repo.go;
  it is not part of the hamr framework itself.
- **hamr dev**: Dev server with file watching, auto-rebuild, and live reload.
  Mirrors its recent output to a rolling log file (default
  `.hamr/dev_logs.txt`, 200 lines) for LLM consumption. The file contains
  `[hamr dev]` messages plus watched command/daemon stdout-stderr with terminal
  escapes stripped. The injected reload script also pipes browser
  `console.*` + uncaught errors + unhandled rejections + resource-load
  failures + CSP violations back to the dev server over a WebSocket at
  `/__hamr/console`; they appear in the same TUI tab and `dev_logs.txt`
  tagged `[site:console]`. Gated by `[dev].hamr_console_capture`
  (default true): when false the WS endpoint isn't mounted and the JS
  doesn't patch console — zero overhead. Browser-engine warnings
  printed directly to DevTools (deprecations, mixed content, autoplay
  blocked, the formatted "Failed to load resource" lines) and
  `fetch`/`XHR` network errors are not catchable from page JS, so they
  aren't captured. Set `[dev].hamr_console_filter = true` to drop
  frames whose message contains `[hamr]` (the reload script's own
  chatter); default false shows everything. Configure via `log_file`
  and `log_file_max_lines` in `[dev]`; set `log_file = "none"` to
  disable. Refuses to start (released
  builds only) when `[hamr] version` in `hamr.toml` is newer than the CLI;
  bypass with `--skip-version-check`. A CLI *newer* than the scaffold only
  warns and continues. Runs as a bubbletea-based TUI. Hotkeys: `r`
  rebuild, `o` open browser, `c` clear active tab's log buffer, `q`/Ctrl+C
  quit, `m` opens a fuzzy palette over the
  project-root `./Makefile` targets (declaration order; type to filter,
  `↑/↓` navigate, `↩` runs, `Esc` cancels; hidden when no Makefile is
  present). Output streams to the hamr tab line-prefixed
  `[make:<target>]`; while running only `q` (cancel — `SIGINT`) and
  Ctrl+C (quit) work; on exit a Done/Failed summary stays until any key
  dismisses it. `Tab`/`Shift+Tab` to cycle
  between the hamr log tab and one tab per `[[dev.docker_compose]]`
  entry (each fed by `docker compose logs -f`), `/` for case-insensitive
  per-tab live (incremental) substring search with `n`/`N` next/prev,
  `f` to toggle a filter view that hides non-matching lines, and `Esc`
  to clear, `?` to toggle a help overlay, and arrows / PgUp / PgDn /
  mouse wheel to scroll the log viewport. Long lines soft-wrap to the
  viewport width and re-wrap on terminal resize. Mouse-click selection:
  click a log line to select the whole logical line (reverse-video, even
  across wrap rows), click+drag extends across multiple lines and
  auto-scrolls when the cursor goes past the top/bottom edge,
  Shift+Click extends from the anchor, Ctrl+Click toggles one line
  in/out (motion under Ctrl is ignored), `y` copies the selection to
  the system clipboard as raw text (ANSI stripped), `Esc` clears the
  selection before clearing search. Selection pauses log auto-scroll
  while held and is dropped on tab switch (indices wouldn't translate).
  Clipboard errors (e.g. missing `xclip`/`wl-copy`/`pbcopy`) surface as
  `[hamr:tui] clipboard: ...` lines on the hamr tab. `q`/Ctrl+C quit
  even when the dev server is parked on a config parse error. `M` toggles
  the MCP gateway (see **hamr mcp**); the status bar shows `MCP on/<n>` or
  `MCP off`.
- **hamr mcp**: MCP (Model Context Protocol) bridge that lets an AI agent
  (Claude Code, Codex, opencode) drive a running `hamr dev`. Two hops:
  agent ⟷ `hamr mcp` over stdio, then `hamr mcp` ⟷ `hamr dev` over localhost
  HTTP at `/__hamr/mcp/{tool}`. Enabled via `[dev.mcp]` (default off) + the
  `M` runtime kill-switch. When enabled the gateway writes a per-run bearer
  token + resolved proxy URL to `.hamr/dev.json` (0600, gitignored); the
  bridge reads it (resolving the project via `--project` or the nearest
  `hamr.toml` from cwd) and authenticates each call. Auth is token-only and
  the namespace is separate from the browser `/__hamr/*` routes, so the
  kill-switch and permissions never affect the dev panel. Permissions are
  per-area in `[dev.mcp.access]` (`read`/`write`/omit=deny; `write` implies
  `read`); areas: `dev` (dev.info), `logs` (logs.read [ANSI-stripped,
  timestamped, rule prefix-matched], console.read [structured timestamped
  frames], http.read [dev-proxy request log: method/path/status/latency,
  incl /__hamr/*, static, SSE]),
  `docker` (logs/status read; restart/wipe write), `mail` (list/get read;
  clear/ingest write), `build` (rule.run, rebuild.all, make.run — write-only),
  `stripe` (list read; complete/expire/refund write). The gateway advertises
  only granted tools and re-checks permission per call. `make.run` is
  constrained by `make_targets` (empty=all) and waits `make_wait` (default
  20s) before returning "still running". Every call is audit-logged to
  `.hamr/mcp_logs.txt` (`log_file`, `"none"` disables); mutations are tagged
  `[mcp]` in the main log; the TUI also has a dedicated `mcp` tab (last in the
  `Tab` cycle) showing one line per request. `hamr mcp install --client claude|codex|opencode`
  (auto-detects when omitted; `--dry-run`) writes the per-client config,
  merging and idempotent — Claude `.mcp.json` + opencode `opencode.json` are
  project-scoped; Codex `~/.codex/config.toml` is global and gets `--project`
  pinned.
- **hamr new**: Project scaffolder. On released builds, queries GitHub for the
  latest release and refuses to run when the local CLI is out of date so
  scaffolds never lag the template; bypass with `--skip-version-check`.
  Network errors warn and fall through; dev builds skip the check.
- **hamr env**: Prints `KEY=VALUE` (or `export KEY=VALUE` with `--export`)
  pairs derived from `.hamr/walks.json` (written by `hamr dev` after
  walking +1 on busy) merged with `.env`. Used in Makefiles / shell scripts
  to pick up walked DB/S3 ports without hardcoding. Exits 0 with no output
  when no walks are recorded — eval is a safe no-op. Match rules: rewrites
  values containing `(localhost|127.0.0.1|0.0.0.0|[::1]):<walked-port>` or
  whole-value `:<walked-port>`. Values relying on scheme defaults (no
  explicit port) are not rewritten.
- **`[dev].port_walk`**: Default true. When a hamr-managed port is busy
  (proxy listen, spawned-app PORT, or any `[[dev.docker_compose]]`
  service's host-published port), hamr walks +1 up to a small cap and
  warns. Rewritten port values are injected into spawned children's env
  AND persisted to `.hamr/walks.json` for `hamr env` / `hamr sync`
  consumers. The scaffold's `.env` is never modified on disk.
- **Compose adoption**: On startup `hamr dev` runs `docker compose ps`
  per `[[dev.docker_compose]]` entry. If every expected service is
  running + ready (healthy or no healthcheck), hamr adopts the stack —
  skips `compose up -d`, leaves the override file alone, and treats
  the project's already-published ports as "owned" so port-walk doesn't
  shift away from them. Anything missing/exited/unhealthy/starting
  triggers the apply path for that entry (walk only the missing
  services, then `compose up -d`). Applies regardless of
  `keep_running` (that flag governs shutdown). Hard-fails on docker
  errors.
- **hamr ai capture**: Browser screenshot capture with HTML/text/metadata sidecars.
  Supports viewport, full-page, and tiled (`--tiles`) capture modes. Tiled mode
  produces viewport-sized PNGs from top to bottom with configurable overlap
  (`--tile-overlap`), ideal for LLM consumption of long pages.
- **hamr ai upgrade**: Git-based scaffold upgrade report. Clones the HAMR repo and diffs between version tags to produce a unified diff + stat summary. Scoped to the surface a project consumes or mirrors — `pkg/` (imported libs), `internal/cli/generator/templates/` (scaffold templates), `docs/` + `llmsdocs/` (the project's own docs/llms context it can bring up to date) — excluding only hamr's internal tooling (CLI/dev-server/generator-logic/binary) and tests.
- **.hamr/ai/**: Playground directory for all AI command artifacts (captures, upgrade reports). Gitignored. Configurable via `[ai] dir` in hamr.toml.
- **hamr locale gen**: Code generator for type-safe i18n accessors.
- **hamr gen static**: Content-based static asset fingerprinting. Reads from
  static/, writes fingerprinted copies to dist/, and generates a Go source file
  with the manifest baked in at compile time. Config in [static] section of
  hamr.toml. `--clean` removes dist/ and resets the manifest.
- **hamr completion install**: Installs shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, or fish. Auto-detects shell from $SHELL. Supports --system for system-wide install (requires root) and --shell to override detection.
- **hamr add skill <target>**: Installs an AI agent skill describing the hamr framework. Target selects the AI tool (currently `claude`). Writes to `./.claude/skills/hamr/` by default; `--global` writes to `~/.claude/skills/hamr/`. Requires `hamr.toml` in CWD for project installs. `--force` overwrites.
- **respond.Redirect**: HTMX-aware redirect that sets HX-Redirect for HTMX
  requests and falls back to HTTP 303.

## Directives

How hamr is meant to be used. Lean rules with rationale, do/don't examples, and tracked gaps where the codebase doesn't yet match.

- `docs/guide/directives/` — directory of directives, one per file
- `docs/guide/directives/gaps.md` — known drifts between code and directives

## Core Packages

### config
Typed accessors for environment variables.
- `GetEnvOrDefault(key, def string) string`
- `GetEnvOrPanic(key string) string`
- `GetEnvOrDefaultInt(key string, def int) int`
- `GetEnvOrDefaultBool(key string, def bool) bool`
- `GetEnvOrDefaultDuration(key string, def time.Duration) time.Duration`
- `GetEnvCSV(key string) []string` — comma-separated list, trimmed, empties dropped
- `ParseBaseURL(raw string) (origin, hostname string, err error)`

### server
Echo wrapper with functional options, graceful shutdown.
- `New(opts ...Option) (*Server, error)` — constructor
- Options: `WithHost`, `WithPort`, `WithDevMode`, `WithMiddleware`, `WithStaticDir`,
  `WithStaticDistDir`, `WithEmbeddedStatic`, `WithErrorHandler`, `WithTimeout`, `WithMaxBodySize`,
  `WithShutdownTimeout`, `WithGeneratedDir`, `WithTrustedProxies` (proxy CIDRs trusted for X-Forwarded-For; unset ignores XFF — behind a proxy you MUST set it to the proxy's range or RealIP returns the proxy IP; never `0.0.0.0/0`)
- Methods: `Echo()`, `Addr()`, `Start()`, `Shutdown(ctx)`, `GET/POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH`,
  `Group(prefix, mw...)`, `StaticPage(path, handler)`, `GenerateStatic(dir)`

### logging
Context-aware structured logging wrapping log/slog.
- `New(production bool) *slog.Logger`
- `FromContext(ctx) *slog.Logger`
- `WithLogger(ctx, l) context.Context`
- `With(ctx, args...) context.Context`

### ctx
Type-safe Echo context key helpers using generics.
- `NewKey[T](name) Key[T]`
- `Set[T](c, key, value)`, `Get[T](c, key) (T, bool)`, `MustGet[T](c, key) T`
- Pre-defined keys: `SubjectIDKey`, `SubjectKey`, `SessionKey`, `RequestIDKey`,
  `FlashKey`, `TranslatorKey`, `LocaleKey`

### respond
HTTP response helpers for HTMX-first applications.
- `HTML(c, status, component) error`
- `JSON(c, status, data) error`
- `Redirect(c, url) error` — HTMX-aware (sets HX-Redirect for HTMX, 303 otherwise)
- `ParsePagination(c, defaultSize) (page, size int)`
- `NewPage(page, size, total) Page`

### validate
Pure-function validators returning "" on success or error message string.
- Core: `Required`, `Email`, `Phone`, `URL`, `PasswordStrength`
- Multi-arg: `MinLength(value, min)`, `MaxLength(value, max)`, `OneOf(value, options...)`
- `IntRange(value, min, max)`, `MinAge(birthDate, minAge)`, `MaxAge(birthDate, maxAge)`
- `CheckPasswordRequirements(password) []PasswordRequirement`
- `NormalizeURL(value)` — prepends "https://" when missing
- `EmptyOr(fn)` — wraps a validator so empty values pass
- Every validator has a `*Msg` variant for custom messages
- `Register(name, fn)`, `Run(name, value)` — custom validator registry
- Character-class: `HasUpper`, `HasLower`, `HasDigit`, `HasSpecial` (composable password rules)
- Curried constructors (return `Rule`): `MinLen(n)`, `MaxLen(n)`, `In(vals...)`, `AgeMin(n)`, `AgeMax(n)`
- `type Rule = func(string) string`, `type CtxRule = func(echo.Context, string) string`
- `WithMsg(rule, msg)` — wraps rule with custom message
- `RunRules(value, rules...)` — runs rules in order, returns first error
- Form API: `NewForm(opts...) Form`, `Form.Validate(c) map[string]string`, `Form.ValidationHandler(paramName) echo.HandlerFunc`
- Field defs: `Field(name, rules...)`, `FieldMsg(name, msg, rules...)` — return `FieldBuilder`
- `FieldBuilder.WithRenderer(fn)`, `FieldBuilder.WithCtx(rules...)`
- Options: `WithOOBRenderer(fn)`, `WithGeneralError(msg)`, `WithTrim(bool)`, `WithShortCircuit(bool)`

### htmx
Request detection and response header helpers for HTMX.
- Request: `IsHTMX(r)`, `IsBoosted(r)`, `GetTrigger(r)`, `GetTarget(r)`
- Response: `Redirect(w, url)`, `Trigger(w, events...)`, `TriggerAfterSettle`,
  `TriggerAfterSwap`, `Reswap(w, strategy)`, `Retarget(w, selector)`,
  `Refresh(w)`, `PushURL(w, url)`, `ReplaceURL(w, url)`

### db
PostgreSQL connection with retry + migrations.
- `Connect(databaseURL, opts...) (*sqlx.DB, error)`
- `ConnectContext(ctx, databaseURL, opts...) (*sqlx.DB, error)`
- Options: `WithMaxOpenConns`, `WithMaxIdleConns`, `WithConnMaxIdleTime`,
  `WithConnMaxLifetime`, `WithMaxRetries`, `WithAttemptTimeout`,
  `WithPgBouncerSafe`, `WithLogger`, `WithOnRetry`
- `Migrate(db, cfg) error`, `MigrateDown`, `MigrateSteps`, `MigrateVersion`, `MigrateForce`
- `StartKeepAlive(ctx, db, interval, _)`, `StartKeepAliveWithConfig(ctx, db, cfg)`

### db/sqlite
SQLite connection + migrations, pure-Go (modernc.org/sqlite). Mirrors pkg/db's API.
- Import path: `github.com/FyrmForge/hamr/pkg/db/sqlite`
- `Connect(path, opts...) (*sqlx.DB, error)` — creates parent dir, applies pragmas, pings
- `ConnectContext(ctx, path, opts...) (*sqlx.DB, error)`
- Defaults: `journal_mode=WAL`, `foreign_keys=ON`, `busy_timeout=5000ms`, `MaxOpenConns=1`
- Options: `WithJournalMode`, `WithForeignKeys`, `WithBusyTimeout`, `WithMaxOpenConns`
- `Migrate(db, cfg) error`, `MigrateDown`, `MigrateSteps`, `MigrateVersion`, `MigrateForce`
- Wraps golang-migrate's pure-Go `database/sqlite` driver (not `database/sqlite3` which requires CGO)
- Scaffolded projects: `DATABASE_PATH` env var (default `./data/<name>.db`); no Docker Compose unless S3 storage is also selected

### middleware
HTTP middleware for auth, CSRF, CORS, flash, security, RBAC, audit, rate limiting, error pages, locale.
- Error Pages: `ErrorPages(defaultPage, overrides...)`, `Page(code, page)` — per-group error rendering
- Auth: `NewBrowserAuth(sm, opts...)`, `Load()`, `RequireAuth()`, `RequireNotAuth()`
- Subject: `GetSubjectID(c) string`, `GetSubject(c) any`, `TrustedSubject()` (unverified header; internal-only), `TrustedSubjectWithConfig(cfg)` (shared-secret/CIDR gated)
- CSRF: `CSRF()`, `CSRFWithConfig(cfg)` (cookie defaults to `SameSite=Lax`)
- CORS: `CORS()`, `CORSWithConfig(cfg)` (no `AllowOrigins` configured = cross-origin denied, not `*`)
- Flash: `Flash()`, `FlashWithConfig(cfg)`, `SetFlash(c, message, flashType)`, `GetFlash(c)`
- Security: `Secure()`, `SecureWithConfig(cfg)` (expanded `SecureConfig` with all header fields), `CacheControl(disableCaching)`, `CacheControlWithConfig(cfg)` (fingerprint-aware: `*.HASH.*` URLs get immutable headers; dynamic responses default to `no-store, private` unless `AllowDynamicCaching`)
- RBAC: `RequireRoles(checker, roles...)`, `RequireActive(checker)`
- Audit: `Audit(logger)`, `AuditWithConfig(cfg)` (sensitive-named query/path param values redacted)
- Rate Limit: `RateLimit(store)`, `RateLimitWithConfig(cfg)`,
  `NewMemoryStore(opts...)`, `NewPGStore(db)`
- Locale: `LocaleFromPath(cfg)`, `LocaleFromPreference(cfg)`,
  `GetLocale(c)`, `GetDirection(c)`

### ptr
Generic pointer helpers.
- `To[T](v) *T`, `From[T](p) T`, `FromOr[T](p, def) T`
- `String(p)`, `Int(p)`, `Bool(p)`, `IntToStr(p)`, `BoolToYesNo(p)`

## Utility Packages

### janitor
Cron-based background task runner.
- `Task` interface: `Name() string`, `Run(ctx) (int64, error)`
- `New(opts...) *Janitor`, `AddTask(schedule, task) *Janitor`
- `Start(ctx) error`, `Stop()`
- Options: `WithTimeout`, `WithLogger`, `WithPreRun`, `WithPostRun`,
  `WithPreTick`, `WithPostTick`, `WithRunImmediately`

### async
Concurrency primitives with panic recovery.
- `All(ctx, fns...) error` — first error cancels remaining
- `Settle(ctx, fns...) []error` — waits for all
- `Map[T,R](ctx, items, fn) ([]R, error)` — concurrent map
- `Fire(fn)` — fire-and-forget goroutine
- `NewGroup(opts...) *Group`, `Go(fn)`, `Close()` — managed goroutine pool
- Options: `WithGroupLogger`, `WithLimit(n)`, `WithMetrics(m GroupMetrics)`
- `GroupMetrics` interface — lifecycle callbacks (Blocked, Dispatched, Completed, Panicked)
- Warning: `GroupMetrics` callbacks are synchronous on the hot path; they must not block, do slow I/O, or panic.

### templint
Static linter for .templ files.
- `New(cfg *Config) *Linter` — creates linter (nil cfg or empty Rules = nothing runs)
- `LintFile(path) ([]Diagnostic, error)`, `LintDir(dir) ([]Diagnostic, error)`
- `FilterBySeverity(diags, minSev) []Diagnostic`, `HasErrors(diags) bool`
- `LoadConfig(path) (*Config, error)` — reads [lint.templ] from hamr.toml as a flat `rule = "warning"|"error"|"off"` map; unknown rule IDs and invalid severities return an error
- `AllRuleIDs() []string`, `DefaultSeverity(id) Severity` — registry helpers
- Rules: `inline-if`, `inline-for`, `inline-switch`, `no-native-form-actions`, `htmx-conflict` (Error default),
  `img-alt`, `no-href`, `inline-style`, `empty-class`, `js-href` (Warning default)

## Optional Packages

### e2e
Reusable go-rod browser helpers for E2E testing.
- `SetupBrowser(t, opts...) *rod.Browser` — launches Chromium, auto-cleanup
- `NewPage(t, browser, url) *rod.Page` — navigates + screenshot on failure
- Actions: `Input(t, page, selector, value)`, `Click(t, page, selector)`,
  `SelectOption(t, page, selector, value)`
- Queries: `ElementExists`, `ElementNotExists`, `ElementText`,
  `ElementAttribute`, `ElementCount`
- Waits: `WaitForElement`, `WaitForURLChange`, `WaitForElementRemoved`
- HTMX: `WaitForHTMXIdle`, `WaitForHTMXSwap`, `ClickAndWaitHTMX`
- Asserts: `AssertElementExists`, `AssertElementContainsText`, `AssertURL`,
  `AssertElementCount`, `AssertElementHasClass`, `AssertURLContains`
- Options: `WithHeadless`, `WithSlowMotion`, `WithTimeout`, `WithArtifactDir`

### auth
Password hashing (Argon2id) + session management.
- `HashPassword(password) (string, error)`
- `CheckPassword(password, encodedHash) (bool, error)`
- `NeedsRehash(encodedHash) (bool, error)`
- `GenerateToken() (string, error)`, `GenerateTokenN(n) (string, error)`
- `NewSessionManager(store, opts...) *SessionManager`
- Session methods: `CreateSession`, `ValidateSession`, `DeleteSession`,
  `DeleteSubjectSessions`, `CookieName`, `Duration`
- `SessionStore` interface: `Create`, `GetByToken`, `Delete`, `DeleteBySubjectID`
- Options: `WithDuration`, `WithCookieName`, `WithCookiePath`, `WithCookieDomain`,
  `WithCookieSecure`, `WithSameSite`, `WithSlidingRefresh`

### i18n
JSON translation files, CLDR plural rules, text/template interpolation.
- `NewBundle(cfg) (*Bundle, error)` — loads locale/*.json files
- `Bundle.Translator(locale)`, `SupportedLocales()`, `HasLocale()`, `ResolveLocale()`
- `Translator.T(key, args...)` — translates with optional count/data
- `FromContext(c) *Translator` — retrieve from Echo context
- `DirectionFor(lang)` — "ltr" or "rtl"

### storage
Pluggable file storage (local filesystem + S3-compatible).
- `FileStorage` interface: `Save`, `Open`, `Delete`, `Exists`, `List`
- `SignableStorage` extends with `SignURL`
- `NewLocalStorage(basePath, opts...)`, `NewS3Storage(cfg, opts...)`

### media
Image/video upload, processing, serving on top of storage.
- `NewLocalImageStore(store, urlPrefix, cfg, opts...)` / `NewS3ImageStore(store, cfg, opts...)`
- `NewLocalVideoStore(store, urlPrefix, cfg, opts...)` / `NewS3VideoStore(store, cfg, opts...)`
- Image upload: `Upload(ctx, fileHeader)`, `UploadFromReader(ctx, r, size)`, `UploadFromReaderWithID(ctx, id, r, size, overwrite)` — id MUST be the 36-char canonical UUID (hyphenless/urn/braced/empty rejected); `overwrite=false` returns `ErrIDExists` (best-effort precheck — concurrent callers can race on Save and on cleanup-after-failure; use DB-level uniqueness for hard exclusion) if the prefix is already populated.
- Video upload: same three methods (`Upload`, `UploadFromReader`, `UploadFromReaderWithID`) with the same UUID/overwrite contract. Thumbnail save failures are logged and ignored.
- Video transcode (opt-in): set `VideoStoreConfig.Transcode VideoTranscodeOptions` at construction and every upload is re-encoded to H.264/AAC MP4 with `+faststart`, `-pix_fmt yuv420p`, `-profile:v high` before saving; zero-valued struct = save bytes verbatim. Activation by population (no `Disable` flag) — even setting only `MaxWidth`/`MaxHeight` activates the full re-encode. validate() fills defaults — `CRF=23`, `Preset="medium"`, `AudioBitrate="128k"`, `MaxWidth/MaxHeight=0` (no clamp); CRF effective range is 1..51 (CRF=0 is treated as "use default", **not** lossless — Go zero value is the sentinel). `MaxSize` is checked against input bytes (pre-transcode); thumbnail still extracted from raw upload; `result.FileSize` reflects on-disk bytes; `result.MimeType` reports `video/mp4` when transcoding ran (regardless of source container), source MIME otherwise. Transcode is slow (tens of seconds on `medium`) — call `Upload*` from a worker pool such as `pkg/async.Group`. `+faststart` requires a seekable output, so the transcode internally writes to an `os.TempDir()` file and reads it back rather than streaming through stdout. The scale filter uses a chained `scale=...,scale=trunc(iw/2)*2:trunc(ih/2)*2` pattern for libx264's even-dimension requirement (works on all ffmpeg versions; avoids `force_divisible_by` which only landed in ffmpeg 4.4).
- `Delete(ctx, id)`, `GetMedia(id)`, `ServeHandler()`
- Sentinels: `ErrFileTooLarge`, `ErrUnknownType`, `ErrVideoTooLong`, `ErrInvalidID`, `ErrIDExists`, `ErrFFmpegNotFound`
- Preset sizes: `SizesAvatar`, `SizesCard`, `SizesIcon`, `SizeOriginal`

### websocket
WebSocket hub with session, subject, and room routing.
- `NewHub(opts...) *Hub`, `Hub.Handler() echo.HandlerFunc`, `Hub.Close()`
- Hub options: `WithSessionIDFunc`, `WithSubjectIDFunc`, `WithOnMessage`, `WithAcceptOptions`, `WithLogger`, `WithSendBufferSize(n)`
- Sending: `SendToSession`, `SendToSubject`, `SendToRoom`, `SendToRoomExcept`, `Broadcast`
- Rooms: `JoinRoom`, `LeaveRoom`
- Events: `NewEvent`, `NewHTMLEvent`, `NewOuterHTMLEvent`, `NewTriggerEvent`
- `NewEmitter(hub)` — typed event wrapper over Hub

### fingerprint
Content-based static asset fingerprinting (SHA-256, 12 hex chars).
- `Fingerprint(srcDir, distDir) (*Manifest, error)` — hash files from src, write fingerprinted copies to dist
- `Clean(distDir) error` — remove the dist directory
- `Manifest.WriteGoManifest(path, pkg) error` — generate Go source with compiled manifest map
- `IsFingerprinted(path) bool` — check if URL path contains a fingerprint hash
- CLI: `hamr gen static [--clean]`, config via `[static]` in hamr.toml

### sync
File-watching and S3-syncing for static assets.
- `SyncAll(ctx, store, dir) error`
- `WatchAndSync(ctx, store, dir) error`

### stripemock (server-side, hosted by hamr dev)
Local Stripe-compatible HTTP backend served on the same proxy mux as `/__hamr/*`. App code uses real `stripe-go/v82`; setting `STRIPE_MOCK=true` points it at the proxy URL (default `http://localhost:3000`). No abstraction layer — `pkg/stripemock` was removed; its surface is now entirely hamr-internal.
- Mock pinned to API version `2025-08-27.basil` (matches stripe-go/v82); CI test asserts the constant equals `stripe.APIVersion`
- Checkout sessions: `POST /v1/checkout/sessions` + `GET /v1/checkout/sessions/{id}`; rejects mixed-currency line items; dev UI at `/__hamr/stripe/checkout?session=<id>` with three outcome buttons (paid / failed / cancelled)
- Connect accounts: `POST /v1/accounts` + `GET /v1/accounts/{id}` + `POST /v1/account_links`; new accounts start in pre-onboarding state (charges_enabled=false, requirements.currently_due populated); dev UI at `/__hamr/stripe/onboarding?account=<id>` with Complete button that flips enabled flags + clears requirements + fires `account.updated`
- PaymentIntents (Connect-aware): `POST /v1/payment_intents` + `GET /v1/payment_intents/{id}` + `POST /v1/payment_intents/{id}/confirm` + `POST /v1/payment_intents/{id}/capture` (manual capture: requires_capture→succeeded, creates Charge, fires pi.succeeded+charge.succeeded[+transfer.created], partial via `amount_to_capture`, exposes `amount_capturable`/`amount_received`); supports `application_fee_amount`, `transfer_data[destination]`, `on_behalf_of`, `Stripe-Account` request header; rejects unknown destinations and `app_fee > amount`; dev UI at `/__hamr/stripe/payment_intent?id=<pi_id>` with Succeed/Fail buttons
- Refunds (Connect-aware): `POST /v1/refunds` (sync-success model matching card refunds) + `GET /v1/refunds/{id}`; caller passes exactly one of `payment_intent`/`charge`; optional `amount` defaults to remaining; `reverse_transfer` updates `Transfer.amount_reversed` and synthesises `source_transfer_reversal` id; `refund_application_fee` flag tracked; updates `Charge.amount_refunded` + `Charge.refunded`; fires `charge.refunded`
- Payouts (Connect-aware): `POST /v1/payouts` + `GET /v1/payouts/{id}` + `GET /v1/payouts` (list scoped by Stripe-Account header — the marketplace dashboard query, honors `?limit=`); manually-triggered start in `pending` (automatic=false); rejects unknown destination accounts; dev UI at `/__hamr/stripe/payout?id=<po_id>` with Mark paid / Mark failed buttons
- Bracket-form decoding for `line_items[0][price_data][currency]=gbp` etc.
- Outcome → real signed webhook (HMAC-SHA256, `Stripe-Signature: t=...,v1=...`) delivered to `webhook_url`; app's existing `webhook.ConstructEvent` verifies as production
- Checkout outcome mapping: paid → `checkout.session.completed` + `payment_intent.succeeded` + `charge.succeeded` (PI + Charge materialised), success_url; failed → synchronous decline, session stays open, no webhook, back to checkout page; cancelled → `checkout.session.expired` + cancel_url
- Account outcome: Complete → `account.updated` event (charges_enabled=true, payouts_enabled=true, details_submitted=true)
- PaymentIntent outcome cascades: succeed (destination charge) → `payment_intent.succeeded` + `charge.succeeded` + `transfer.created` (auto-creates Charge + Transfer; transfer amount = pi.amount - app_fee); succeed (no destination) → PI + charge events only; fail → `payment_intent.payment_failed` only (no synthesised charge, mirrors real sync card declines)
- Refund cascade: synchronous success on POST → fires `charge.refunded` async with the post-refund Charge state; reverse_transfer increments the Transfer's amount_reversed (capped at remaining unreversed balance)
- Payout outcomes: Mark paid → status=`paid` + `payout.paid` event; Mark failed → status=`failed` + populates `failure_code`/`failure_message` + `payout.failed` event
- Race-safety: 409 Conflict on double-submit (checkout + account); 410 Gone on stale completed-session reload
- Wired by `hamr new --stripe`: scaffolded `main.go` calls `stripe.SetBackend(...)` pointing at `HAMR_STRIPE_MOCK_URL` (auto-injected by `hamr dev` from `[proxy].listen`) when `envStripeMock` is true; refuses to start with `STRIPE_MOCK=true && !DEV_MODE` so a leftover dev flag in prod can't silently route real payments to localhost; `.env` and `hamr.toml` get a generated `whsec_dev_<hex>` shared secret
- Config via `[dev.stripe]` in `hamr.toml`: `enabled`, `webhook_url`, `webhook_secret` (required when enabled), `persist` (default true), `persist_path` (default `.hamr/stripe/state.json`); requires `[proxy]` since both API and UI live on the proxy mux
- Persistence: single JSON file atomically rewritten on every state mutation; loaded on `hamr dev` startup; survives restart so sessions/PIs/accounts/refunds/payouts don't vanish on every config tweak (useful for LLM workflows that read prior state)
- Dashboard at `/__hamr/stripe`: 5 tables (sessions/accounts/PIs/refunds/payouts) newest-first, per-row actions: "Resend webhook" (re-fires natural events), "Refund" form on succeeded PIs (full or partial, with reverse_transfer toggle), "Expire" on open sessions; `hamr dev` panel shows an "Open Stripe mock" shortcut button when the mock is enabled (sse_config.stripe_mock flag)
- Not yet mocked: standalone Transfer create, Customer, Price, Subscription, Invoice, Dispute, GET endpoints for Charge/Transfer/ApplicationFee
- Logging: mock-emitted lines tagged `[hamr:stripe]` (separate from `[hamr dev]`) via a `component=stripe` slog attr that hamr's devHandler interprets and strips; `grep '\[hamr:stripe\]'` to filter
- Dev/staging — production leaves `STRIPE_MOCK` unset and stripe-go talks to api.stripe.com

### email
Provider-agnostic `Sender` interface for outbound transactional email. Apps depend on this interface; concrete impls (real provider adapters, emailmock, SMTP) plug in at startup.
- `Sender` interface: `Send(ctx context.Context, msg Message) (*Result, error)` — must be concurrency-safe
- `Message` fields: `From`, `To/Cc/Bcc []Address`, `ReplyTo *Address`, `Subject`, `Text`, `HTML`, `Attachments`, `Inline []Attachment`, `Headers`, `Tags map[string]string`
- `Address{ Name, Email string }`, helper: `Addr(name, email string) Address`
- `Attachment{ Filename, ContentType string; Data []byte; ContentID string }` — ContentID set for Inline (referenced as `cid:<ContentID>` in HTML)
- `Result{ ID string }` — provider-assigned message id, may be empty
- Provider-specific features (templates, per-recipient substitutions, scheduled send, tracking) live in `Headers`, `Tags`, or the app's own adapter — not the interface

### emailmock
Dev-only `email.Sender` implementation. JSON-POSTs `email.Message` to the hamr dev server's `/__hamr/mail/ingest`, which stores it in an in-memory inbox viewable at `http://<dev-proxy>/__hamr/mail`. Does NOT construct MIME — validates app use of `email.Sender`, not provider wire format.
- `New(baseURL string) *Client` — baseURL is the hamr dev proxy origin (trailing slashes stripped)
- `Client.Send(ctx, msg) (*Result, error)`
- Magic recipients at any local-part match short-circuit storage: `bounce@*` → `ErrBounced`, `reject@*` → `ErrRejected` (checked across To/Cc/Bcc, case-insensitive)
- `ErrBounced`, `ErrRejected` — exported sentinels for `errors.Is`
- Request timeout: 10s
- Dev-only — no production guards; gate on `EMAIL_MOCK=true` in main.go
- Inbox UI: newest-first list + substring search, tabbed detail view (HTML sandboxed iframe, Plaintext, Raw RFC822-ish, Headers, Attachments), outcome simulation (mark failed/delayed), mobile viewport toggle, images-blocked toggle
- Configured server-side via `[dev.email]` in hamr.toml (enabled, max_messages default 500, max_message_bytes default 10 MiB, persist default true, persist_path default `.hamr/mail/inbox.mbox`). Requires `[proxy]` section — inbox UI lives on the proxy mux
- Persistence: inbox is mirrored to a standard MBOXO mbox file so it survives `hamr dev` restart. File opens in Thunderbird/mutt/Apple Mail. Set `persist = false` for ephemeral in-memory-only inbox. X-Hamr-* headers round-trip runtime metadata (status, tags, received-at, id) invisibly to real mail clients
- HTTP ingest: `POST /__hamr/mail/ingest` with JSON `email.Message` — returns `{ID}` on 200, `{error:"bounced"}`/`{error:"rejected"}` on 422, `{error:"message too large"}` on 413
- CSRF: mutating POST endpoints reject cross-origin requests (Origin header set and ≠ Host). Absent Origin passes (curl, tests). Inbox is unauthenticated — don't bind the proxy to a public interface
